# Bylaws: what needs to be covered

## California baseline

California nonprofit public benefit corporations should adopt bylaws early after formation.

Key code sections:

- Corp. Code § 5150: https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=CORP&sectionNum=5150
- Corp. Code § 5151: https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=CORP&sectionNum=5151.
- Corp. Code § 5310 (members or no members): https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=CORP&sectionNum=5310.
- Corp. Code § 5211 (board quorum basics): https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displaySection.xhtml?lawCode=CORP&sectionNum=5211

What the bylaws should cover at minimum:

- whether the corporation has members or no members;
- number of directors or method for setting that number;
- board meetings, notices, and quorum;
- officer roles and authority;
- committees;
- conflict procedures / approval mechanics;
- indemnification;
- amendment process.

## Recommended structure for gecon.ai

The simplest structure is usually:

- `no members`;
- a fixed initial board of `3` directors or a range with board authority to set the exact number;
- standard officers: President, Secretary, Treasurer;
- most directors independent from paid staff.

California law permits one or more directors, but for a credible charity, three active directors is usually the safer governance setup.

## IRS-sensitive bylaw topics

The bylaws should reinforce, but not replace, the articles.

Recommended provisions:

- corporation operates exclusively for 501(c)(3) purposes;
- no private inurement;
- no substantial lobbying and no campaign intervention;
- conflict of interest policy adoption/reference;
- board approval and documentation for compensation;
- dissolution process that is consistent with the articles.

IRS governance references:

- Form 1023 instructions: https://www.irs.gov/instructions/i1023
- IRS conflict policy explanation: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/form-1023-purpose-of-conflict-of-interest-policy

## Practical bylaw checklist

- `No members` clause included.
- Board size mechanism included.
- Director terms and removal process included.
- Meeting notice and quorum rules included.
- Officer titles and appointment/removal rules included.
- Committee authority limited appropriately.
- Conflict of interest policy adopted or incorporated.
- Fiscal year specified.
- Indemnification language reviewed.
- Amendment authority specified.
